metadata.paper_title: An Evaluation of Software Test Environment Architectures

system_context.system_description: An interactive test environment for Prolog programs that partially automates test execution, test development, and test measurement.
system_context.business_goals: Provide a fully automated test environment for Prolog programs.

evaluation_setup.evaluation_goal: To demonstrate SAAM's analytical value by comparing three software test environments and evaluating architectural constraints on test artifact reusability.

architectures.name: PROTest II
architectures.description: Composed of five components: a test driver, structure checker, test input generator, test coverage analyzer, and a test report generator.

key_components.responsibilities: Generates test inputs based on test coverage.
Analyzes instrumented source code to determine clause coverage.

scenarios.scenario_text: Reuse of test artifacts (test plans, test scripts, test suites, test cases, test criteria, test oracles, test results) managed by the STE.

scenario_classification.rationale: Requires architectural support for test management and specific structural separation which is found to be lacking or requiring modification in some evaluated systems.

scenario_evaluations.change_description: Add test management capabilities and decouple test measurement from test development.
issues_identified: Lack of provision for test management (no artifact persistence).
Functional interdependency among structural components inhibits reuse (test inputs generated based on coverage).

architectural_strengths: Focus on defining coverage in real logic programming terms.
architectural_weaknesses: Lack of structural separation between test development and test measurement.
Interdependency of test measurement with test development and test execution.
Does not support test failure analysis.

architecture_comparison.rationale: Does not support test artifact reuse; architecture is highly coupled and lacks test management.

missing_information: Specific stakeholders involved in the evaluation beyond the authors.
Detailed breakdown of estimated effort for changes.
inference_notes: Inferred 'Indirect' classification for the reuse scenario as the paper discusses it as a quality that is supported or impeded by the architecture, implying potential changes needed for support.
